name: 20 MG Duloxetine Gastro-Resistnt Tablets IP
about: 20 MG Duloxetine Gastro-Resistant Tablets IP are a type of general medicine that are used to treat various mental health disorders such as depression, anxiety, and panic attacks. These tablets come in a solid form and have to be taken orally with water. They are produced in India and are available in a convenient packaging that can be stored in a dry place. The active ingredient in these tablets is duloxetine which helps in regulating the levels of serotonin and norepinephrine in the brain. This, in turn, helps in improving mood, reducing anxiety, and managing pain caused by certain conditions such as fibromyalgia and neuropathic pain. If you are prescribed 20 MG Duloxetine Gastro-Resistant Tablets IP, it is important that you take them as directed by your healthcare provider. Do not crush or chew the tablets and do not stop taking them abruptly without consulting your healthcare provider.

faq.q: What is the recommended dosage for 20 MG Duloxetine Gastro-Resistant Tablets IP?
faq.a: The recommended dosage for these tablets is one tablet per day, taken orally with water.

faq.q: What are the possible side effects of these tablets?
faq.a: Some common side effects of these tablets include nausea, dry mouth, constipation, and dizziness. If you experience any severe side effects, contact your healthcare provider immediately.

faq.q: Can these tablets be taken during pregnancy?
faq.a: It is important to consult your healthcare provider before taking these tablets if you are pregnant or planning to become pregnant.

faq.q: Are there any food restrictions while taking these tablets?
faq.a: There are no specific food restrictions while taking these tablets. However, it is recommended to avoid alcohol while taking these tablets.

faq.q: Can these tablets be taken with other medications?
faq.a: It is important to inform your healthcare provider about all medications you are taking before starting these tablets. Some medications may interact with 20 MG Duloxetine Gastro-Resistant Tablets IP and cause adverse effects.
